Dept. 514; Judge Murlene J. Randle; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:50:57 a.m. and concluding at 12:09:35 p.m.

Plaintiff Qi Mou Yee represented by Stephanie Foster, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.

Court orders matter continued to Dec-08-2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for recipt of proposed order and final judgment. If proposed order and final judgment are received, appearance is not required.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Xiao Hua Kuang and Zhou Zhang.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Stephanie Foster, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Qi Mou Yee and Yim Fong Wong, as Trustees of the Yee/Wong Family Trust, U.D.T., dated August 2, 2011.
Court Certified Cantonese speaking interpreter assisted plaintiffs Qi Mou Yee and Yim Fong Wong during this morning's proceedings.
Plaintiffs, Ms. Wong and Ms. Foster all appeared via ZOOM for today's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Xiao Hua Kuang or Zhou Zhang.
Plaintiffs' Complaint for Partition of Real Property, and Accounting, was filed on September 30, 2019.
Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank filed its Answer to plaintiffs' complaint on November 12, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Zhou Zhang occurred on October 6, 2019, and was effectuated on October 16, 2019.
Notice of Pendency of Action was filed on December 16, 2020.
Defendant Xiao Hua's Answer to plaintiffs' complaint was filed on December 26, 2019.
Default as to defendant Zhuo Zhang was entered on January 28, 2020.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on the parties on March 23, 2020, setting trial for October 19, 2020.
Stipulation for Entry of Judgment as to defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank was filed on August 20, 2020.
Order continuing trial to February 1, 2021 was filed with the Clerk's Office on October 14, 2020. On February 1, 2021, Department 514 continued the matter to February 8, 2021. Due to inadequate notice, the matter was continued to today, February 22, 2021.
DOE defendants dismissed today, February 22, 2021 through oral motion. Plaintiffs' counsel to file Request for Dismissal with the Clerk's Office, forthwith.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Qi Mou Yee and Yim Fong Wong, as Trustees of the Yee/Wong Family Trust, U.D.T., dated August 2, 2011, and against defendant Xiao Hua Kuang, and defaulted defendant Zhuo Zhang, as follows:

(1) Plaintiffs Qi Mou Yee and Yim Fong Wong, as Trustees of the Yee/Wong Family Trust, U.D.T., dated August 2, 2011, are the sole owners of the real property more commonly know as 1450-1452 47th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94122 ("the Property");
(2) The rights, shares and interest of the parties in the Property are as follows:
(a) Plaintiffs are 50% owners of the Property.
(b) Defendant Xiao Hua Kuang is a 25% owner of the Property.
(c) Defendant Zhuo Zhang is a 25% owner of the Property.
(d) The Property is owned by plaintiffs and defendants as tenants-in-common, each with an undivided interest in the Property.
(3) There is an outstanding line of credit secured by the Property held by Bank of America in the amount of $17,671.15;
(4) There is an outstanding mortgage loan secured by the Property held by JP Morgan Chase Bank in the amount of $595,833.23; and
(5) Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 873.010, Kevin Singer is appointed Referee, with full authority to manage and partition, by sale, the Property.

Matter is CONTINUED to May 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report re: Status of Sale of the Property and Entry of Final Judgment.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Interlocutory Judgment for Partitiion of Real Property and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Qi Yee's Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Responses To Demands For Inspection And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Interrogatories and Demands for Inspection is unopposed and granted. Defendant shall serve verified responses within fifteen days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $960.00 is awarded plaintiff as against defendant to be paid within the aforesaid fifteen days.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.
